nodes.context: Yakuza criminal organization based in Okinawa, Japan, with estimated membership 210-270.
Splinter group of Kyokuryu-kai, formed in 1990.
Largest known yakuza syndicate, attempted to expand into Okinawa.
One of two gangs that merged to form Kyokuryu-kai in 1970.
One of two gangs that merged to form Kyokuryu-kai in 1970.

edges.evidence_quote: the Kyokuryu-kai is one of the two designated yakuza groups in Okinawa Prefecture along with its splinter group Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai
edges.context: Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai is a splinter group that broke away from Kyokuryu-kai in 1990.

edges.evidence_quote: From 1973 to 1981, the Kyokuryu-kai was in fierce conflict with the Yamaguchi-gumi
edges.context: Kyokuryu-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi were in a violent conflict from 1973 to 1981.

edges.evidence_quote: The Kyokuryu-kai was formed in 1970 as the Okinawa-rengo Kyokuryu-kai by two gangs, the Naha Clan and the Koza Clan.
edges.context: Naha Clan merged with Koza Clan to form Kyokuryu-kai in 1970.

edges.evidence_quote: The Kyokuryu-kai was formed in 1970 as the Okinawa-rengo Kyokuryu-kai by two gangs, the Naha Clan and the Koza Clan.
edges.context: Koza Clan merged with Naha Clan to form Kyokuryu-kai in 1970.

edges.evidence_quote: The Kyokuryu-kai had been in conflict with the Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai for years
edges.context: Kyokuryu-kai and Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai were in conflict for years after the split.
